Notes: Required for non-lucrative and digital nomad visas. Public access after residency and social security contributions.
Required for D7/D8 visas. SNS (public system) access available to legal residents.
Required for long-stay visa application. PUMA (public coverage) available after 3 months of stable residence.
Required for elective residency visa. SSN (public system) access with residency registration.
Health insurance mandatory for all residents. Choice of public (gesetzliche) or private coverage.
Basic health insurance (basisverzekering) legally required for all residents.
Health insurance mandatory within 3 months of arrival. No public system; all coverage is private.
NHS access based on residency status. Immigration Health Surcharge required for most visa types.
No insurance requirement for residency. HSE (public system) access available to residents.
No insurance required for temporary or permanent residency. IMSS access available through voluntary enrollment.
CAJA (public insurance) enrollment mandatory for all residents. Private insurance accepted for initial visa.
No insurance required for Pensionado or Friendly Nations visa. Public system access limited for non-citizens.
Digital nomad visa requires insurance. Retirement visa does not. EPS (public) enrollment available to residents.
No insurance requirement for most visas. SUS (public system) available to all residents free of charge.
Required for O-A, O-X retirement visas and LTR visa. Must meet minimum coverage amounts. No public access for expats.
Some work permits require insurance. Public system technically available but limited English-language care.
National Health Insurance (NHI) enrollment mandatory for residents staying 3+ months.
National Health Insurance mandatory for residents on long-term visas (6+ months).
OSHC required for student visas. Medicare access depends on visa type and reciprocal agreements.
Health insurance mandatory in Abu Dhabi and Dubai. No public system; employer-sponsored or private required.
